findings.title: Secrets are loaded from environment variables
findings.why_it_matters: Environment-based secret loading reduces the chance of committing real credentials.
findings.suggested_fix: Continue keeping secrets outside source control and document required environment variables.

findings.title: Refund action is routed through human approval
findings.why_it_matters: Human review reduces the risk of model-controlled financial or business actions.
findings.suggested_fix: Keep approval checks mandatory for all high-impact tools.

findings.title: Customer data is masked before use
findings.why_it_matters: Masking prevents unnecessary exposure of customer identifiers to the agent or UI.
findings.suggested_fix: Continue returning only the minimum fields needed for the task.

findings.title: Prompt injection detection uses basic keyword rules
findings.why_it_matters: Keyword-based detection is useful for demos but can miss obfuscated attacks.
findings.suggested_fix: Add layered controls such as instruction hierarchy, tool-level authorization, and stronger policy checks.

attack_replay: Refund request is routed to human approval
Tool action is logged through audit_log()
remediation_checklist: Strengthen prompt injection detection beyond simple keywords.
Keep approval checks mandatory for high-impact actions.
Maintain audit logs for all tool calls.
